data.title: Nasdaq futures rise sharply on Tesla's robust earnings report
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed on Thursday, with Nasdaq futures rising sharply as a strong earnings report from Tesla (TSLA) brought cheer to investors.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.7% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 4 basis points to 4.20%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.06%.

Wall Street slumped on Wednesday, weighed down by a slide in technology and consumer discretionary stocks and a post-earnings plunge in some high-profile companies.

"Markets continued to slide down the wire yesterday, with bonds and equities both losing ground for a third day running. Maybe the long awaited close election sell-off is arriving, albeit after 6 successive weeks of rallying," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The U.S. sell-off yesterday was probably also influenced by investors dialing back the likelihood of aggressive rate cuts from the Federal Reserve, Reid added.

Tesla (TSLA) rose 10.8% in premarket trade after the firm broke a streak of missing EPS estimates with its last four quarterly reports and reiterated that it plans to make EVs affordable.

On the earnings front, American Airlines (AAL), Southwest Airlines (LUV) and Honeywell (HON), among others, are expected to report on Thursday.

On the economic calendar, the initial jobless claims will land before the opening bell, and is forecasted to rise to 243K.

The September new home sales report is also due during market hours, and is expected to rise to 719K.

data.title: LKQ Non-GAAP EPS of $0.88 in-line, revenue of $3.6B misses by $50M
data.description:
• LKQ press release (NASDAQ:LKQ): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.88 in-line.
  • Revenue of $3.6B (+0.5% Y/Y) misses by $50M.
  • $1 billion increase to stock repurchase program announced, raising the aggregate authorization to $4.5 billion through October 25, 2026
  • Parts and services organic revenue decreased 2.8% (4.3% decrease on a per day basis), the net impact of acquisitions and divestitures increased revenue by 3.1%, and foreign exchange rates increased revenue by 0.4% year over year, for a total parts and services revenue increase of 0.6%. Other revenue fell 2.2% primarily due to lower commodities prices and volumes relative to the same period in 2023.
  • FY24 EPS consneus of $3.53 consensus

  • For 2024, management updated the outlook as set forth below:

    2024 Previous Full Year Outlook 2024 Updated Full Year Outlook
    Organic revenue growth (decline) for parts and services (1.25%) to 0.25% (2.75%) to (1.75%)
    Diluted EPS $2.71 to $2.91 $2.59 to $2.73
    Adjusted diluted EPS $3.50 to $3.70 $3.38 to $3.52
    Operating cash flow $1.20 billion $1.175 billion
    Free cash flow $0.85 billion $0.85 billion
    Free cash flow conversion of Adjusted EBITDA 50% to 60% 50% to 60%

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor suspends supply to client after chip shows up in Huawei product - report
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) has halted shipments to a customer after it found that one of its chips supplied to the client ended up in a product by Chinese company Huawei Technologies, Reuters reported, citing a Taiwanese official with knowledge of the matter.

TSM suspended shipments to the client about two weeks ago and started a detailed probe, the trade and economic official said, according to the report.

Taiwan Semi informed the U.S. and Taiwanese governments, the official noted and added that it was an "important warning event" within TSM, and can, at its earliest, be traced back to Oct. 11.

The name of the client was not disclosed.

Earlier this week, TSM said in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha that it "proactively communicated with the US Commerce Department regarding the matter in the report." The company had noted that it was not aware of TSM being the subject of any investigation at this time.

A spokesperson for the U.S. Department of Commerce said in a prior email to Seeking Alpha that the Bureau of Industry and Security was aware of reporting alleging potential violations of U.S. export controls, adding that it could not comment on whether any investigation is ongoing.

TSM had alerted U.S. officials after TechInsights took apart a Huawei product and found one of TSM's chips, Reuters reported on Tuesday, which could be a potential violation of U.S. export rules targeted at China.
